Off Calendar Dec-16-2021 Set For Trial On Dec-05-22 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, Or Alternatively, Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention
Off Calendar Sep-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Laurie S. Julien of Berkes Crane Robinson & Seal LLP (213-955-1150), appearing via CourtCall for SPECIALLY APPEARING INTERVENOR GREAT AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY. No other appearances on this matter.

Ruling:
The Court received late notice from the moving party of its intent to contest the tentative ruling. Therefore the Court did not hear any oral argument and adopted its tentative ruling:
Specially Appearing and Proposed Intervenor Great American Insurance Company's Motion to Vacate and Set Aside Default and Default Judgment is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Proposed Intervenor's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A and B. (Evidence Code section 452(c).) The request for judicial notice is denied as to Exhibit C.

The September 26, 2017 default judgment entered against dissolved corporation C.F. Bolster Company is set aside solely as related to Great American Insurance Company. The request to set aside the May 13, 2015 entry of default against C.F. Bolster Company is denied. Great American Insurance Company may not file an answer on behalf of C.F. Bolster Company. (see W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Ct., (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1209.) Great American Insurance Company is granted leave to file an answer in intervention within 10 days of the entry of order on the instant motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee; Matter Not Reported (503/CML)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Carroll Duncan Co., C.F. Bolster Company, E.S. Browning Co., And K R Automotive
Off Calendar Sep-21-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Gail Dekreon; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Janine M. Fiel-Cosse, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Ysidro Limon.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Caroll Duncan & Co., C.F. Bolster Company, E.S. Browning Co., or K&R Automotive.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
Default of Defendants has been duly entered.
Court grants Plaintiff's oral motion to dimiss Defendant Does 1-800. Plaintiff to file dismissal after hearing.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against defendants as follows:

In favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant Carroll Duncan & Co.:
Economic Damages $78,061.04
Non-Economic Damages $750,000.00
For a total of $828,061.04

In favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant C.F. Bolster Company:
Economic Damages $78,061.04
Non-Economic Damages $750,000.00
For a total of $828,061.04

In favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant E.S. Browning Co.:
Economic Damages $78,061.04
Non-Economic Damages $750,000.00
For a total of $828,061.04

In favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant K&R Automotive:
Economic Damages $78,061.04
Non-Economic Damages $750,000.00
For a total of $828,061.04
For a total Judgment in the amount of: $3,312,244.16
Plaintiff to submit a Judgment reflecting the Court's orders.
